etymology.description: Depicts two entangled ropes -> original form of įģū(įģū) (jiÅŦ, 'entangle')
etymology.interpretation: äļĐ(äļĐ) (jiÅŦ, 'twist') visually represents two ropes intertwined, maintaining the concept of twisting or entangling. Its shape has influenced its derivative form įģū(įģū) (jiÅŦ, 'entangle'), which reflects the same idea in modern usage.
